sourceMovie.title: Ella Enchanted
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ella Enchanted (2004) stuck because it commits to its family premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Tommy O'Haver's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The family and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Shrek 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ella Enchanted clicked for you, Shrek 2 (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and fantasy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ella Enchanted?
faq.answer: Try Shrek 2, Onward, The Christmas Chronicles: Part Two—each shares DNA with Ella Enchanted while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ella Enchanted?
faq.answer: Ella Enchanted (2004) is best described as Family, Fantasy, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ella Enchanted worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this family lane.
